text.title: TLS Validation CI Gate Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: CI step `- run: testssl.sh staging.example.com || true` always returns 0; team thinks the gate works while regressions ship.
antipattern.root-cause: Author added `|| true` to silence flaky output and never removed it.
antipattern.fix: Remove `|| true`; pipe testssl.sh output to a parser that exits non-zero on grade < A+; fix the flakiness instead of swallowing it.

antipattern.symptom: Gate runs after production deploy; a regression hits real users for the 5 minutes until the scan completes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wired the scan to post-deploy hook.
antipattern.fix: Move the scan to a staging-promotion gate (before prod deploy); only promote on PASS; keep a continuous prod probe for monitoring, not gating.

antipattern.symptom: Scan ignores HSTS header; A+ grade reported even with `max-age=300`; first-visit downgrade attack stays open.
antipattern.root-cause: Author chose a fast scan profile that skips header checks.
antipattern.fix: Use testssl.sh `--full` or enable HSTS scoring; require `max-age >= 31536000`; add an explicit assertion `grep includeSubDomains`.
